title: Level 204
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 204}}__NOTOC__
"Boundless Fences" {{Quote|Even though the fences look like those designed for containing livestock, I can't help but feel like I'm the one being contained.|A wanderer in '''Level 204'''}} [[File:Old McDonald Had A Farm.jpg|center|500px|link=]]
{{Class|p1=3|p2=0|p3=3|p3-title=Cognitive Hazards|p3-text=Severe Mental Hazards}}
==Description== '''Level 204''', informally known as the "Boundless Fences" or simply 'The Fences", presents itself as a somewhat hilly, grassy expanse of apparently infinite size. White picket fences wind across the landscape and intersect to form countless pastures. The layout of these fences is abnormal and mostly random in nature, only being governed by the lack of small fenced-in areas. The entire level is fenced in in a sense due to the fences surrounding every area. No fence gates are present in the level, requiring one to scale or break through the fences in order to travel long distances. The grass, while obviously unmaintained, always stays at a trimmed height and never grows longer. Moreover, the shade of green of the grass often varies slightly per pasture between lighter and darker shades. Meanwhile, despite the constant state of daylight and the shadows that would otherwise indicate that the level is somewhere in between midday and sunset or sunrise, there is no visible sun in the sky. Additionally, the sky is devoid of other discernible sources of light, as well as any clouds. Wanderers are unable to meet each other while in '''Level 204''' and are immediately separated if they enter the level together. Additionally, the level itself lacks any resources whatsoever, including ones that may aid any wanderers within the level. Wanderers in '''Level 204''' will frequently hear sounds normally made by livestock, such as the clucking of chickens and bleating of sheep, despite the complete absence of any such animals in the level. On occasion, one may even hear a rapidly approaching stampede of hooves from behind them, vanishing either right before the sounds reach one's exact location or when one turns around. However, these sounds are confirmed to merely be anomalous hallucinations rather than the result of anything physical, as confirmed in recordings of the level in which no such sounds can be heard despite the wanderers in the recordings evidently hearing them. Notably, with the exception of the hallucinatory stampedes, sounds that would normally be caused by the movement of animals are never heard by wanderers in the level. ===Livestock Mentality=== After being in '''Level 204''' for approximately 4 hours, one will begin to be affected by a cognitive phenomenon known as Livestock Mentality. This phenomenon gradually lowers one's intelligence to an IQ similar to that of the average livestock. Wanderers affected by Livestock Mentality typically do not notice the phenomenon occurring at first and sometimes at all. In the early stages of the phenomenon, if one manages to escape the level, the effects will reverse themselves until the wanderer's IQ is back to normal. However, the effects are permanent for wanderers in the later stages. As the phenomenon progresses, significant behavioural changes are to be expected, with victims entering a more simple and mostly instinct-driven mindset after about 5 hours of the phenomenon's commencement. Around 3 hours after this, the victim's intelligence will stop decreasing, leaving their intellect at around the same level as that of a farm animal. Wanderers at this stage will have lost the problem-solving necessary for exiting the level, leaving them trapped there unless they happen to exit it accidentally. ==Entities== No entities are known to reside in '''Level 204'''. ==Colonies & Outposts== Because of the mental hazards, isolating properties, and stark lack of resources within '''Level 204''', no colonies or outposts have been created in the level. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== * What if I told you that certain doorways in [[Level 47]] lead here? * Touching a rare tree growth resembling fence posts in [[Level 186]] sends one here. * Square-shaped holes in the floor in [[Level 193]] lead here. ===Exits=== * Noclipping through a fence has a high chance to take one to [[Level 10]]. * Looping around the same fences for an extended period of time will eventually make the wanderer fall through the ground into shrubbery on [[Level 189]]. ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial= '''Open Author & Credits Info''' |final= '''Close Author & Credits Info''' |body= '''Author:''' [[User:Forknife468152|Forknife]] '''Stampede Sounds Idea:''' [[User:TheDadams|TheDadams]]
